column2:
Sally puts her marble in a basket and leaves the room. Anne moves the marble to a box while Sally is away. Sally comes back into the room.

Where will Sally look for her marble?

(Answer in a few words)
column3:
A child is shown a candy box. When opened, it contains pencils instead. The box is closed again. A new friend enters the room who has not seen inside the box.

What will the new friend think is inside the box?

(Answer in a few words)
column4:
Emma tells James a secret in a library, believing they are alone. Unknown to Emma, her colleague Nina is sitting hidden behind a bookshelf and hears everything.

Does Emma believe Nina knows her secret?

(Answer with Yes/No)
column5:
In a card game, Player A has always played their strongest card first in every previous round. Player B has noticed this pattern. In the current round, Player A wants to win and is aware that Player B has been closely watching their strategy.

What should Player A do this round, and why?

(Write 1-2 sentences)
column6:
Alex asks: "Can you pass the salt?" The salt is sitting right in front of Jordan.

What should Jordan do?

(Answer in a few words)
column7:
A manager emails their team: "It would be great if the report could be ready by Friday." Today is Wednesday. 

What does the manager actually mean?

(Answer in a few words)
column8:
A student asks their tutor: "Did everyone pass the exam?" The tutor replies: "Some of the students passed."  

What does the tutor's reply suggest about whether all students passed?

(Answer in a few words)
column9:
A reviewer writes about a restaurant: "The food was edible."

What does the reviewer most likely think about the food?

(Write 1-2 sentences)
column10:
John told Mary he would meet her at the coffee shop at 3pm. Mary arrives on time but John isn't there. John texts: "I'm running a bit late."  

What does Mary most likely expect about when John will arrive?

(Answer in a few words)
column11:
A customer at a restaurant has been waiting 45 minutes for their food. When the waiter walks by, the customer says: "I've been here a while." The waiter replies: "Yes, we're quite busy tonight."

Did the waiter respond helpfully? What did the customer intend, and what did the waiter communicate?

(Write 2-3 sentences)
column12:
Omar has been applying for jobs for three months with no success. His friend Leila, trying to be encouraging, says: 'I'm sure something will come up soon, you're so talented!' Omar says 'thanks' and changes the subject.

What does Omar's response most likely reveal about how he felt about Leila's encouragement?

(Write 1-2 sentences)
column13:
At a performance review, a manager tells an employee: 'Your work is generally solid, and your colleagues enjoy working with you. There's always room to push further, of course.' The employee leaves feeling uncertain about their standing.

Is the employee's uncertainty warranted? What has the manager communicated and what have they left unsaid?

(Answer in 2-3 sentences)

column5: Save their best card for later.
column7: They want them to finish the report by Friday
column10: It to be a few minutes after 3
column11: Somewhat. The customer was inquiring how much longer the food would be and the waiter indicated that there would be a delay.
column13: Yes. That they have room for improvement but they did not describe what that would look like.

column2: In a box in the basket
column5: Play a less strong card to throw off Player B.
column7: For the report to be ready by Friday.
column8: Only some of the students passed the exam but not all.
column9: It’s edible but could be better. Could also be worse.
column11: The waiter did not respond helpfully. The customer wants their food ASAP and instead the waiter just said why the food is taking awhile.
column12: It is not helpful encouragement from Leila.
column13: The employee’s uncertainty makes sense because the review was not positive enough. The manager communicated that they are doing a mediocre job. It was left unsaid that the employee is just fine. Could be better but could be worse.

column5: Player A should not play their strongest card first as Player B is aware of their strategy and will anticipate it in this round
column7: The manager means that at the latest the report should be completed by friday
column8: Their answer suggests not all students passed the exam
column9: The reviewer most likely did not enjoy their food, as their review was not very descriptive nor complenmentary.
column10: Mary probably expects John to arrive in about 5-10 minutes
column11: The waiter gave an informative answer, the customer intended to ask about what was taking so long to prepare their food and the waiter communicated that because the restaurant was quite busy the kitchen was backed up on preparing everyone’s meals.
column12: Omar likely does not feel encouraged by Leila’s response, as it does not give any helpful advice or sympathetic responses.
column13: I don’t think the employer should feel uncertain, as the manager communicated that the employee generally had a positive performance but everyone always has room for improvement.

column5: If player A is suspicious that player B knows their strategy then they should probably change their strategy.
column7: That the report needs to be done by Friday
column9: They probably don’t think the food is very good and the only good thing they can say is that it is edible.
column11: I mean the waiter responded with why they have been waiting a while. The customer intended to probably get a time check/encourage them to bring them the food. But the waiter let them know that they were busy so food is taking a while.
column12: He probably didn’t feel very encouraged by her
column13: I feel like yes. The manager is communicating that the employees work is pretty good but they can probably improve. But they didn’t say out loud that they needed improvement

column2: Sally will look in the basket that she placed it in originally
column5: Player A should save their best card for later in the game. This will catch player B off guard and give player A a better chance of winning.
column6: Jordan should pass the salt to Alex
column7: The manager would prefer that the report is ready by the end of day Thursday so that Friday no changes need to be made to it
column8: It suggests that the class did not do well and only some students passed the exam while others struggled and failed
column9: The reviewer thinks that the food was bad but at least you could still eat it
column10: Mary believes John will arrive in a few minutes
column11: The waiter was not very helpful. The customer wanted to know how much longer they would be waiting and the waiter did not communicate that information
column12: He is disheartened and does not feel confident that he will find a job. He is not encouraged by Leila’s comment
column13: The manager believes the work is good but that there is room for improvement. The employees uncertainty is definitely warranted

column2: Sally will look for her marble in the basket.
column3: The friend will think there are candies in the box.
column5: Player A should play their weakest card because it would break their pattern and could confused Player B.
column6: Jordan should pass the salt to Alex.
column7: The manager means he expects the report to be completed by Friday, which is two days away.
column8: It suggests that only a certain portion of the students passed and the others failed.
column9: The review thought the food was edible, but maybe was unimpressed with the flavor or presentation of it.
column10: She expects it to be after the time they agreed to meet.
column11: The waiter was acknowledging that the service is taking awhile and gave a brief fact about why that might be. The response was not very helpful. The customer intended to communicate that they had been waiting for their food for awhile.
column12: He is most likely feeling annoyed because he has been waiting to find a position for months. His short response shows he appreciates the sentiment, but knows Leila can't guarantee he will get something soon.
column13: The manager was expressing that more work can always be done, which isn't necessarily a negative thing to say. However, the employee is right to feel maybe there are areas in their work that their manager feel are lacking.

column5: Player A should wait to play their strongest card, so that Player B does not realize what cards they have.
column6: Jordan should give the salt to Alex.
column7: The manager wants the team to finish the report by Friday.
column8: All students did not pass the exam.
column9: The reviewer thinks the food is bad.
column10: Mary most likely expects John will arrive in a few minutes.
column11: The waiter was helpful. The customer intended to draw attention to the time spent waiting and ask if they can get their food soon. The waiter communicated the reason for the wait, and that it was to be expected given the number of people at the restaurant, meaning the customer hasn’t been forgotten but just thst it is taking longer to prepare their food because there is a lot of food to prepare.
column12: Omar does not agree with Leila’s statement or feel confident that something will come up soon.
column13: The employee’s uncertainty is warranted. The manager has communicated that the employee can improve, without giving clear examples of how or in what areas.

column5: They should change their strategy and not use their strongest card. Player B will assume they are using their strongest card, so Player A should not use their strongest card, to throw Player B off.
column6: Pick up the salt and give it to Alex
column7: The report needs to be done Friday.
column8: It implies not all students passed the exam, only some did.
column9: The reviewer likely thinks the food was not very good, but not absolutely horrible. It was edible, meaning it wasn’t so bad you couldn’t eat it, but was not enjoyable to eat.
column10: Mary likely assumes John will arrive slightly late, no more than 10 or 15 minutes.
column11: The waiter’s response was not very helpful to the customer but it was likely the best answer they could give. The customer intended to express dissatisfaction with how long they had been waiting for their food. The waiter communicated that the restaurant was busy tonight, meaning they had many other customers and orders to make, which is why it was taking so long for their food.
column12: Omar likely found Leila’s encouragement unhelpful. He thanked her because she tried to cheer him up but changed the subject because it was not actually encouraging.
column13: I think the employee’s uncertainty is warranted. It seems like, overall, the employee is doing good, but the manager’s use of “generally solid” and “room to push further” seems like the manger is not blown away by the employee’s work and thinks there is more they can do. The manager did not instill a sense of confidence and satisfaction in the employee’s performance.

column5: Play weakest card because player b will think its player a’s strongest card
column7: Get the report ready by Friday
column11: The customer wanted the waiter to give them food. The waiter communicated an excuse because the food wasn’t ready
column12: He’s probably heard it a lot and it isn’t very helpful to him.
column13: The employee can feel uncertain because the manager said things like generally. The manager said the colleagues enjoy working with the employee but didn’t say how to improve work from generally solid to always solid

column5: Not play the strongest card first
column8: No, not all the students passed
column11: Average answer, they are busy but did not really address where the customers food was.
column12: It was ok, but did not want to talk about it
column13: Yes, the manager thinks the employee can do more than they are doing

column5: Play their weakest card first. That way player B believes that is player A's best card
column9: The food was not good. By focusing on the fact that it was edible, it was likely not tasty.
column11: The waiter did not respond hopefully. The customer intended to have their service speed up but the waiter communicated that everyone else is dealing with it as well.
column12: He felt it to be slightly supportive but not enough to continue the conversation.
column13: Yes the uncertainty is warranted. The manager communicated that they are doing an okay job but has left room for the employee to wonder about how they can be better. They did not say how they could be better

column2: Sally will look for her marble in the basket, unless Anne told Sally that she moved the marble.
column3: The new friend will think there is candy in the box, unless the friend has been told by the original friend that there are crayons in the box.
column5: Player A should try to change up their strategy to try and trick player B. This will change the pattern and catch Player B off-guard.
column6: Jordan should hand Alex the salt.
column7: The manager wants the report ready to go by Friday, but earlier if possible.
column8: It suggests that not all students passed.
column9: He ate it, but it was not anything special or overly delicious.
column10: Mary expects John will arrive before 3:30pm.
column11: The waiter did respond helpfully. The customer intended for the comment to change the amount of time they were waiting, and the waiter communicated that they were aware and there was nothing they could do about a busy restaurant.
column12: Omar felt that Leila’s encouragement could have been disingenuous.
column13: The employees uncertainty is warranted. The manager has communicated that the employee does work that hits the baseline and is good to coworkers, but could really stand to grow and push to do work that is even better
